stories.headline: A.J. Brown Rides East to New England, and the Whole Camp Shakes
stories.read: Well pardner, it finally happened, the Eagles shipped their star gun A.J. Brown clear across the country to New England, and young Drake Maye is the fella feedin' him now. That is still a top-shelf No. 1 receiver, and Maye led the whole territory in accuracy last fall, so keep Brown saddled as one of your first guns, no flinchin'. The dry well is Romeo Doubs, who just watched a quarter of the throws ride out of town, so knock him down your board. And back in Philly every leftover pass-catcher inherits a fatter cut of the haul, so pan those creeks before the saloon wakes up.

stories.headline: Bucky Irving's Busted Wing Mended, He's Full-Go for Camp
stories.read: Good smoke off the Tampa wire, pardner: Bucky Irving says his busted wing has healed clean and he'll break camp July 29 without a hitch. Trouble is, the Bucs are talkin' about splittin' the reins three ways with Kenneth Gainwell and Sean Tucker, so don't go countin' on the whole haul. He is still the lead horse and touches pay the rent, but you're payin' top dollar for a back who might share the load. Ride him, just don't reach three rounds early expectin' a workhorse.

stories.headline: Tucker Kraft Opens Camp Laid Up, but He's Gushin' Gold by Week 1
stories.read: Here's your diamond in the rough at tight end, pardner. Kraft opens camp laid up on the mend list nursin' a busted knee, which has got the saloon passin' him right over, but the Packers swear he'll be full-go by Week 1. Before he took his spill last fall he was the top tight end in all the land, fourteen points a game. That is elite gold at a spooked-down price, so strike that vein while the herd is still frettin' over a slow start.
